token_family_docs.$description: Fully resolved semantic color tokens — neutral, interaction, status, free, and brand. No mode resolution required. Use these tokens in component and HTML token files. Do: Use when assigning color values in component and HTML token files. Compiled tokens are fully resolved — mode handling is integrated. Avoid: Do not reference S1 tokens directly in components — S1 is a bridging layer where mode handling is partial. Always use compiled tokens.

Official federal identity color (Bundesrot) Do: Use for the primary brand accent in navigation active states and official federal identity elements. Avoid: Do not use for interactive states or status feedback — use the interaction or status scale instead.

brand.$description: Static color to be used in the brand relevant UI elements.

token_family_docs.$description: Foundational colors for backgrounds, text, borders, and surfaces. Do: Use for text, background, border, and shadow roles in standard UI components. Avoid: Do not use for interactive state feedback — use the interaction scale. Do not use for status communication — use the status scale.

no_color.$description: Static value when no color respectively 0% opacity is needed.

token_family_docs.$description: Interactive state colors for components. Both Lightness (Light/Dark) and Emphasis (High/Low) modes affect resolved values across all 4 columns. Do: Use for interactive component states — buttons, links, form controls, and focusable elements. Avoid: Do not use for static decorative elements. Do not use for status communication — use the status scale instead.

token_family_docs.$description: Status communication colors. No emphasis modes — only Light/Dark modes apply. Contrast levels (e.g. contrast_highest) are available. Do: Use for alerts, badges, and any element that communicates system or process state. Avoid: Avoid for decoration or branding — status colors carry fixed semantic meaning. Do not use for interactive states — use the interaction scale.

token_family_docs.$description: Free colors. No emphasis modes — only Light/Dark modes apply. Contrast levels (e.g. contrast_highest) are available. Do: Use for tags, badges, and contextual color accents where no fixed semantic role applies. Suitable for categorical distinction. Avoid: Do not substitute for status or interaction colors. Free tokens carry no predefined semantic meaning — assign meaning in context.
